mission.title: falco: Get pod data from Kubelet daemon or from container runtime.
mission.description: Get pod data from Kubelet daemon or from container runtime.. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**Motivation**

It is possible that the k8s api is inaccessible. Then falco won't be able to augment the logged events with k8s entities data (like pod name, namespace etc.).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Get pod data from Kubelet daemon or from container runtime.
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Get pod data from Kubelet daemon or from container runtime." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the linked issue and PR for the community-verified solution.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
